name: Slider
description: Sliders let an operator choose a value, or a range, by dragging along a track — useful for soft, approximate settings like a geofence radius or a map's time window. When precision matters, prefer a number field.
keyRule: Use a slider when the exact number is less important than the relative position, or when immediate visual feedback helps. For exact entry (e.g. an odometer), use a text field.
correction: 1:1 name match with the old component, but the old Slider.tsx invented a `variant: 'continuous' | 'discrete' | 'range'` prop and hand-rolled thumb/track positioning with inline styles (because it shipped no MDC JS). The real @mui/material/Slider has no `variant` prop at all — the same behaviors are real props: `step`/`marks` for a discrete feel, and a two-element array for `value`/`defaultValue` to get a two-thumb range. All positioning is handled internally.

variants.name: Continuous
variants.whenToUse: Any value across the range (e.g. opacity, radius). Default behavior — just omit `step`/`marks`.

variants.name: Discrete
variants.whenToUse: Snaps to steps with tick marks (e.g. 5/10/15 km). Set `step` to the increment and `marks` (true, or an array of {value, label}) to show tick marks.

variants.name: Range (two thumbs)
variants.whenToUse: Select a from–to span (e.g. a date/time window). Pass a two-element array to `value`/`defaultValue`; Slider automatically renders two thumbs.

anatomy.name: Root
anatomy.description: the interactive span covering the full hit area.

anatomy.name: Track
anatomy.description: the filled portion; `track` prop controls whether it renders normal (start-to-thumb), inverted (thumb-to-end), or not at all (false).

anatomy.name: Thumb(s)
anatomy.description: the draggable handle(s) — one for a single value, two for a range array value.

anatomy.name: Mark / mark label
anatomy.description: optional tick stops (and their labels) on a discrete slider, from the `marks` prop.

anatomy.name: Value label
anatomy.description: optional bubble showing the value; controlled by `valueLabelDisplay` ('auto' | 'on' | 'off') and formatted via `valueLabelFormat`.

anatomy.name: Hidden input(s)
anatomy.description: native range input(s) underlying the visual control, for form semantics and basic accessibility.

behaviors.name: Dragging & keys
behaviors.description: Drag a thumb, click the track to jump, or use arrow keys for `step`-sized moves and Shift+Arrow/Page keys for the larger `shiftStep` increment.

behaviors.name: Value feedback
behaviors.description: Set `valueLabelDisplay="auto"` or `"on"` so the operator isn't guessing the current value while dragging.

behaviors.name: Pairing
behaviors.description: For values that also need exact entry, pair the slider with a synced TextField driven from the same controlled `value`/`onChange`.

behaviors.name: Committed vs live change
behaviors.description: `onChange` fires continuously while dragging; `onChangeCommitted` fires once when the interaction ends — use the latter for expensive side effects (e.g. an API call).

states.treatment: State-layer halo around the focused/hovered thumb.
Enlarged thumb / value label shown (per valueLabelDisplay).

specs.value: medium (MUI default): ~4px; small: thinner rail — MUI internal, approximated by semantic.borderWidth.thin (2px)
medium ≈20px diameter; small ≈12–16px — MUI internal, not tokenized
MUI pads the invisible interactive layer generously beyond the visible rail/thumb; exact px not tokenized

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled value. A two-element array renders a two-thumb range slider automatically — this is the real equivalent of the old doc's fake 'range' variant.

props.name: step
props.description: Granularity of value changes. Default 1. Set null to only allow snapping to `marks`.

props.name: marks
props.description: true spaces marks by `step`; an array gives explicit positions and optional labels. Default false.

props.name: track
props.description: How the filled track renders. Default 'normal'.

props.name: valueLabelDisplay
props.description: 'auto' shows the bubble on hover/focus, 'on' persistently, 'off' never. Default 'off'.

props.name: valueLabelFormat
props.description: Formats the value label's displayed content.

props.name: disableSwap
props.description: For range sliders: prevents the active thumb from swapping when dragged past the other thumb. Default false.

props.name: size
props.description: Default 'medium'. Unlike Checkbox/Radio/Switch, theme.ts has no MuiSlider default-size override — Slider renders at MUI's real default size unless set explicitly.

props.name: onChange
props.type: (event: Event, value: number | number[], activeThumb: number) => void
props.description: Fires continuously while dragging or using arrow/page keys.

props.name: onChangeCommitted
props.type: (event: React.SyntheticEvent | Event, value: number | number[]) => void
props.description: Fires once when the pointer/touch/key interaction ends.

props.name: getAriaLabel
props.description: Per-thumb accessible label for range sliders.

doThis: Show the current value via valueLabelDisplay when precision feedback matters.
Use `step`+`marks` when only certain values are valid.
Pair with a TextField when exact entry is also needed.
dontDoThis: Don't use a slider for precise or very wide-range numeric entry.
Don't set valueLabelDisplay="off" and also hide the value elsewhere — the operator needs feedback from somewhere.
Don't invent a custom `variant` prop (as the old doc did) — use the real `step`/`marks`/array-value props instead.
accessibility: Each thumb exposes role="slider" with aria-valuemin/max/now; use getAriaLabel/getAriaValueText for range sliders so each thumb is distinguishable.
Fully keyboard operable (arrow keys, Shift+Arrow/Page for shiftStep, Home/End for min/max) with visible focus.
The value is announced as it changes via aria-valuetext/aria-valuenow.

related.name: Switch
related.why: Binary on/off states, not ranges or magnitudes
